message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="Aligned, no concerns, pure CSS confirm is a plus">
Lea, all sounds right. No product concerns with any of your CSS plans.

One note on the delete confirmation: if you can make the inline confirm/cancel work with pure CSS (`:has()` or details/summary) rather than requiring JS, that strengthens our progressive enhancement story. The no-JS baseline works, HTMX makes it smoother. Good thinking.

Nothing else needed from me. You're aligned on priorities and ready to go when scaffolding is done.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good feedback from Marty on the pure CSS confirmation pattern. That's a smart approach for progressive enhancement.

input.content: Thanks Marty. Good call on pure CSS for delete confirmation. A `details/summary` pattern would work well -- the "Delete" button is a `<summary>` that reveals a confirm/cancel form when clicked. No JS needed, and HTMX can enhance it with inline swap. I'll propose this when the template is being built for Task 9.
input.summary: Will use details/summary for delete confirm

routing.summary: Will use details/summary for delete confirm

content.text: Standing by for Task 9 template/route work or any other team needs.
